user_brief: Create an original square repository logo for an open-source non-commercial project named 'Ansys AEDT MCP Server'. Do not copy or imitate the official Ansys logo or any trademark. Visual concept: abstract electromagnetic field lines, simulation mesh, small connected MCP nodes, and a subtle desktop engineering tool motif. Professional technical style, clean vector-like raster, high contrast, modern GitHub project branding, works at small avatar size, no visible text, transparent background if supported. Use a palette of deep graphite, electric cyan, signal amber, and white highlights.
research_queries: Ansys Electronics Desktop official product overview site:ansys.com
Ansys Electronics Desktop unified platform electromagnetic simulation site:ansyshelp.ansys.com
Model Context Protocol server visual icon nodes abstract logo

research_notes.point: Ansys Electronics Desktop (AEDT) is described by Ansys as a platform for electronics system design and access to EM solvers such as HFSS, Maxwell, Q3D Extractor, SIwave, and Icepak.
AEDT is characterized in Ansys documentation as a unified environment where electromagnetic, thermal, and circuit designs can be created, simulated, and post-processed.
research_notes.source: Getting Started with Ansys Electronics Desktop (ansyshelp.ansys.com)

research_notes.point: Ansys developer documentation states AEDT supports scripting/automation through ACT Python, PyAEDT, and related libraries.
research_notes.source: Ansys Developer Portal electronics and semiconductors guide (developer.ansys.com)

research_notes.point: Ansys has trademark usage guidelines; official marks should not be copied or imitated in derivative branding.
Ansys broadly positions its products around physics-based simulation and engineering workflows across electronics and multiphysics.

visual_strategy.use_case: Square GitHub repository avatar/logo for an open-source engineering tooling project
visual_strategy.audience: Developers, simulation engineers, automation users, GitHub visitors
visual_strategy.core_message: A professional, compact emblem that suggests electromagnetic simulation, engineering automation, and connected server orchestration without using any trademarked brand identity

original_elements_to_preserve: Abstract electromagnetic field visualization language inspired by AEDT context
Simulation mesh or faceted solver geometry
Unified desktop engineering tool feeling without copying any UI
Automation/network node cues consistent with MCP server tooling
Strictly avoid copying or imitating the official Ansys logo, mark, or trademark color/layout language
style_or_poster_language_to_reference: Minimal, bold silhouette for small-avatar legibility
inferred_creative_additions: A central geometric core wrapped by luminous field arcs
3–5 small connected nodes integrated into the perimeter or core
A faint abstract window/panel frame suggesting a desktop engineering tool
A restrained mesh overlay to imply simulation without clutter
A subtle server/network structure that reads well at 32x32 and 64x64

image_prompt.primary_request: Create an original square repository logo for an open-source non-commercial project named Ansys AEDT MCP Server, as a clean technical emblem that suggests electromagnetic simulation, mesh-based solver geometry, small connected MCP nodes, and a subtle desktop engineering tool motif, while remaining fully original and not resembling any official Ansys branding.
image_prompt.scene_backdrop: Transparent background if supported, otherwise a neutral deep graphite backdrop with no scenery, no environment, no extra objects
image_prompt.subject: A centered abstract engineering icon: a bold faceted geometric core or shield-like module, wrapped by 2–4 luminous electromagnetic field arcs and contour-like waves, with a sparse simulation mesh overlay and 3–5 small connected MCP nodes arranged as a compact network constellation; include only a faint schematic window-frame or panel outline as a subtle desktop tool cue
image_prompt.composition_framing: Square avatar composition, strong central focal point, thick readable silhouette, balanced symmetry with slight asymmetry for energy, clear negative space, designed to remain legible when downscaled to 32x32 and 64x64
image_prompt.style_medium: Original clean vector-like raster logo, crisp edges, high-detail but simplified for icon legibility, modern technical branding, minimal and geometric, no photorealism, no illustration clutter
image_prompt.lighting_mood: High contrast with luminous cyan and amber accents, white highlight points on node junctions and field intersections, subtle glow only, crisp and controlled rather than flashy
image_prompt.color_palette: Deep graphite base, electric cyan primary glow, signal amber secondary accents, white highlights, dark neutral shadows, no pastels
image_prompt.materials_textures: Smooth matte graphite body, precise glowing vector-like linework, sparse mesh facets, subtle metallic or glassy tech impression, clean polished surfaces without realism-heavy texture
image_prompt.typography_layout: No visible text, no letters, no numbers, no wordmarks, no labels, no UI copy
constraints: Original icon only; do not copy or imitate the official Ansys logo or any trademarked branding
No visible text, letters, numbers, or symbols that read as typography
Keep the design readable at small avatar sizes
Prioritize a strong central silhouette and simple geometry
Use abstract electromagnetic field lines, not literal scientific diagrams
Include only a subtle desktop engineering tool motif, not a full UI screenshot
Use a square composition and centered emblem
Maintain a professional open-source GitHub repository logo aesthetic
Keep the palette limited to deep graphite, electric cyan, signal amber, and white highlights
Avoid clutter and preserve crisp vector-like edges
avoid: Any resemblance to the official Ansys logo, color blocking, or brand identity
Visible text, wordmarks, or tiny unreadable labels
Overly complex circuitry or dense meshes that collapse at small size
Cartoon, playful, or sci-fi fantasy styling
Photorealism, 3D render look, or noisy gradients
Irrelevant objects, people, devices, landscapes, or office scenes
Messy typography, low detail, incoherent anatomy, or weak composition
Soft pastel palettes or generic app-icon clichés

final_generation_prompt: Create an original square GitHub repository logo for the non-commercial open-source project 'Ansys AEDT MCP Server' that clearly feels like a professional engineering/simulation tool emblem while remaining fully original and not resembling the official Ansys logo or any trademark. Center a bold abstract geometric core with 2–4 luminous electromagnetic field arcs and contour-like wave lines, a sparse simulation mesh or faceted solver geometry, and 3–5 small connected MCP nodes integrated into the perimeter or core. Add only a very subtle schematic desktop engineering tool motif such as a faint window-frame or panel outline, not a literal UI screenshot. Style it as a clean vector-like raster icon with crisp edges, high contrast, minimal clutter, and strong readability at 32x32 and 64x64 sizes. Use a limited palette of deep graphite, electric cyan, signal amber, and white highlights. Transparent background if supported, otherwise neutral dark background. No visible text, no letters, no numbers, no wordmarks, no extra scenery, no photorealism, no cartoon style, and no resemblance to official Ansys branding.
